beat.title: Ambush in the Pit
beat.boundaryReason: The session opens with the party sliding into the arena chamber, the beast calling up living snow around each hero, and the group scrambling through the first exchange while they identify the encounter and try to stabilize their positions.
dateEvidence: session.yaml sets drStart to 1730-01-25
the party resumes directly from the prior cliffhanger with no sleep, camp, or other day transition before initiative
nothing in the opening combat setup suggests a change from the same continuous delve

beat.title: Turning the Echoes
beat.boundaryReason: The party finally decodes the living snows enough to start killing them efficiently, with Justas recasting Spirit Guardians, Eolo's attacks landing cleanly, and the fight swinging away from survival mode toward control of the chamber.
dateEvidence: the combat continues seamlessly from the prior beat in the same arena with no interruption
the only progression is tactical momentum within the same encounter, not a change in day

beat.title: Through the Earthen Exit
beat.boundaryReason: After the beast dies, the room sheds its frost, Kalima panics toward the new tunnel, the party grabs only a few facts and trophies, and the scene shifts completely when they crawl out onto the cliffside overlooking Melusa and dispatch the fae scout toward the ice palace.
dateEvidence: the party emerges from the tunnel into a scene the DM explicitly frames as late afternoon
the tunnel crawl takes only about ten minutes, keeping this beat on the same day as the combat
there is still no sleep, long rest, or next-morning cue after leaving the labyrinth chamber

beat.title: House of Endurance
beat.boundaryReason: Inside the safe house, the party learns how Melusa understands Kestavo, sacrifice, sunset, and survival, while the villagers reveal just enough of their cosmology and fears for the players to realize how deeply isolated the settlement has become.
dateEvidence: the villagers describe the sun as still hanging in its strange prolonged cycle, and the environment remains locked in late-afternoon light
the conversation flows directly from arrival and continues before the gong-driven sleep cycle begins
although session.yaml ends on 1730-01-26, the transcript has not yet shown an on-stage transition into the next day

beat.title: Portals and Night Watch
beat.boundaryReason: Once the gong rings, the session turns into layered reconnaissance: Ekko scouts the village and the guarded mirror-gash, Finnan uses clairvoyance to inspect the palace and the forming ice bridge, and the party ends by choosing to hide, rest, and face the consequences tomorrow.
dateEvidence: the gong marks the village's sleep cycle and the scene explicitly shifts into night-time planning
the party discusses bedding down, taking a long rest, and picking things up tomorrow, but no next-morning action occurs on stage before the session ends
keeping the beat on 1730-01-25 matches the transcripted events even though session.yaml allows the overnight span to extend into 1730-01-26
